skill.name: error-handling-gate
skill.description: Verify errors are handled gracefully with meaningful user feedback. Issues result in WARNINGS.
skill.summary: Verify errors are handled gracefully with meaningful user feedback. Issues result in WARNINGS.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptography patterns, but manual review found these are Markdown text and prose false positives. The skill contains a checklist, review prompts, and illustrative code examples; no executable scripts, network behavior, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Blocks
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the referenced lines are fenced Markdown templates and illustrative JavaScript examples. They are documentation examples and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are Markdown fence delimiters, placeholder file references, or sample catch blocks. No shell command invocation or Ruby backtick execution is present in executable code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography terms, but the referenced lines contain normal English words such as description, handles, modes, and codes. No cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these lines are prose checklist content. There is no DES, MD5, SHA1, or similar weak cryptographic operation.

content.value_statement: Unhandled failures create confusing user experiences and make production issues harder to debug. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a focused error handling review.
actual_capabilities: Reviews async operations for missing try/catch, catch handlers, and cleanup paths.
Checks whether user-facing error messages are clear, useful, and non-technical.
Prompts reviewers to examine logging, debugging context, and sensitive data exposure.
Identifies common error handling issues such as empty catch blocks and stuck loading states.
Provides PASS and WARNING response templates for consistent review outcomes.
limitations: It is a review guide and does not automatically modify source code.
It depends on the assistant having access to the relevant code paths.
It does not replace runtime testing, monitoring, or incident response planning.
It focuses on application error handling, not infrastructure resilience.

use_cases.title: Pre-merge error handling review
use_cases.description: Run a focused check before merging changes that add forms, network calls, or async workflows.

use_cases.title: User experience failure audit
use_cases.description: Evaluate what users see when save, load, login, or checkout operations fail.

use_cases.title: Production debugging readiness check
use_cases.description: Confirm that errors include useful context without exposing sensitive data in logs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic error review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the error-handling-gate skill to review this change for missing error handling and confusing user feedback.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single pull request or feature branch.

prompt_templates.title: Async workflow review
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the error-handling-gate skill to every async operation in this feature. Report PASS or WARNING with locations and user impact.
prompt_templates.scenario: Reviewing forms, data loading, or third-party API calls.

prompt_templates.title: Production readiness review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the error-handling-gate skill to check failure scenarios, logging context, sensitive data exposure, and recovery options.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing a feature for release or operational handoff.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-path failure audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform an advanced error-handling-gate review across the main success path, validation failures, network failures, partial failures, and concurrent requests.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deep review of a complex user workflow.

output_examples.input: Review a profile save flow that calls an API and shows a loading spinner.
output_examples.output: WARNING: The review finds that the spinner can remain visible after a failed save. It also recommends a clear retry message for the user.

output_examples.input: Check a data loading feature before release.
output_examples.output: PASS: The feature handles network failures, logs enough context for debugging, clears loading state, and shows a helpful message.

output_examples.input: Audit checkout failure handling for partial payment and inventory failures.
output_examples.output: WARNING: The review asks for separate messages for payment, inventory, and network failures, plus logging that avoids sensitive payment data.

best_practices: Review the main failure modes before judging the quality of the error handling.
Pair each warning with user impact and a concrete location when possible.
Check both user feedback and production debugging visibility.
anti_patterns: Approving empty catch blocks because the success path works.
Showing raw exception messages directly to end users.
Logging sensitive request or user data while adding debugging context.

faq.question: Does this skill fix error handling automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides review and produces PASS or WARNING feedback. A developer still applies the code changes.

faq.question: Can it review frontend and backend code?
faq.answer: Yes. The checklist applies to both, as long as the relevant code is available for review.

faq.question: What kinds of issues does it look for?
faq.answer: It looks for missing catches, silent failures, poor user messages, stuck loading states, weak logging, and missing recovery paths.

faq.question: Is this a security skill?
faq.answer: It is mainly a coding quality skill. It also reminds reviewers to avoid leaking sensitive data in logs or error messages.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before merging features that rely on async operations, external APIs, forms, payments, or important user workflows.

faq.question: Does it require special tools?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown-based review guide compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
